summary:
One-way ANOVA should be used because it can effectively analyze whether significant differences exist among group means based solely upon varying degrees of smoking impact on lung function measured through average peak flows; other tests are either too simplistic or inappropriate due to focusing incorrectly on binary comparisons instead of multi-category analysis pertinent herein. Thus,

The answer corresponds most accurately under Option \[A]. Agent2 concluded using *Option* \(A\) which refers specifically towards employing "One Way" ANalysis Of VAriances (\(F-test)\). This choice was justified considering its applicability over simpler methods lacking sufficient complexity needed while also avoiding unnecessary multivariate analyses beyond necessary scope presented
